Al Jazeera English.ownership: Owned by Al Jazeera Media Network, funded by the Qatari government.
Al Jazeera English.notes: Launched 2006; editorially independent per its charter, but state-funded; widely read international news outlet.

Middle East Eye.ownership: Privately held; founded by David Hearst in 2014. Funding sources have not been publicly disclosed. Qatari financial links have been alleged by critics; MEE denies state funding.
Middle East Eye.notes: UK-based online outlet focused on the Middle East and North Africa; known for critical coverage of Israel and Gulf states.

Haaretz.ownership: Majority owned by the Schocken family. M. DuMont Schauberg, a German publisher, holds a minority stake.
Haaretz.notes: Founded 1919; Israel's oldest daily newspaper; English and Hebrew editions; known for liberal-critical coverage of government.

Haaretz (English).ownership: Majority owned by the Schocken family. M. DuMont Schauberg, a German publisher, holds a minority stake.
Haaretz (English).notes: English-language edition of Haaretz, Israel's oldest daily newspaper. Known for liberal and critical coverage of Israeli government policy.

The Guardian.ownership: Owned by Scott Trust Limited, a not-for-profit trust established to preserve the Guardian's editorial independence in perpetuity. No commercial owner.
The Guardian.notes: Founded 1821; UK-based; one of the most widely read English-language news websites globally; center-left editorial stance.

BBC News.ownership: Publicly funded; governed by the BBC Royal Charter. Funded primarily through the UK television licence fee paid by UK households.
BBC News.notes: Founded 1922; world's largest public broadcaster; editorially independent from government by charter, though funding is subject to periodic government review. Bias rating: center-left per Ad Fontes Media (AllSides rates it center; Ad Fontes methodology is quantitative and journalist-scored).

Reuters.ownership: Owned by Thomson Reuters Corporation, a publicly traded Canadian-American media conglomerate.
Reuters.notes: Founded 1851; one of the world's largest international wire services.

Reuters (via GV Wire).ownership: Reuters content syndicated by GV Wire, a local California news outlet. Reuters is owned by Thomson Reuters Corporation.

Euronews.ownership: Majority owned by Alpac Capital, a pan-European investment group, since 2022. Previously partly owned by NBCUniversal.

Ynet News.ownership: Published by Yedioth Communication Ltd, part of the Yedioth Ahronoth group — Israel's largest media conglomerate, controlled by the Mozes family.
Ynet News.notes: Online news arm of Yedioth Ahronoth, Israel's highest-circulation newspaper. Center to center-right; one of the most visited news sites in Israel.

The Times of Israel.ownership: Independent, privately held. Founded by David Horovitz; no disclosed majority external owner.
The Times of Israel.notes: English-language Israeli newspaper founded 2012; center to center-right editorial stance.

Times of Israel.ownership: Independent, privately held. Founded by David Horovitz; no disclosed majority external owner.
Times of Israel.notes: English-language Israeli newspaper founded 2012; center to center-right editorial stance.

Jerusalem Post.ownership: Owned by Riesling Investment & Technology Ltd, controlled by Israeli investor Eli Azur. Previously owned by Miriam Adelson.
Jerusalem Post.notes: Founded 1932 (as the Palestine Post); English-language Israeli daily; center-right editorial stance; large English-speaking diaspora readership.

i24 News (Israel).ownership: Owned by Altice Group, controlled by French-Israeli billionaire Patrick Drahi.
i24 News (Israel).notes: Multilingual international news channel launched in 2013 from Tel Aviv; broadcasts in English, French, and Arabic; center-right Israeli perspective.

i24 News.ownership: Owned by Altice Group, controlled by French-Israeli billionaire Patrick Drahi.
i24 News.notes: Multilingual international news channel launched in 2013 from Tel Aviv; broadcasts in English, French, and Arabic.

Arutz Sheva (Israel National News).ownership: Operated by a non-profit association registered in the United States. Closely associated with the national-religious settler movement in the West Bank.
Arutz Sheva (Israel National News).notes: Founded 1988 as a pirate radio station; now a digital outlet. Strongly right-wing Zionist editorial stance; primary outlet for the Israeli settler community.

Arutz Sheva.ownership: Operated by a non-profit association registered in the United States. Closely associated with the national-religious settler movement in the West Bank.
Arutz Sheva.notes: Founded 1988; strongly right-wing Zionist editorial stance; primary outlet for the Israeli settler community.

Israel Hayom.ownership: Founded by Sheldon Adelson in 2007; now owned by the Miriam and Sheldon G. Adelson Family Foundation following his death in 2021. Distributed free of charge.
Israel Hayom.notes: Israel's highest-circulation daily newspaper. Strongly pro-Netanyahu editorial stance; its free distribution model was controversial, with competitors alleging it distorted the market.

Bloomberg.ownership: Privately owned by Michael Bloomberg and Bloomberg LP partners.
Bloomberg.notes: Founded 1990; financial and general news with global reach; subscription-based.

Truthout.ownership: Independent non-profit; funded by reader donations. No corporate or government ownership.
Truthout.notes: Founded 2001; progressive news and opinion outlet.

The Jewish News (Detroit) / JNS.ownership: Jewish News is an independent Jewish community newspaper based in Michigan. JNS (Jewish News Syndicate) is a non-profit wire service for Jewish and pro-Israel media.
The Jewish News (Detroit) / JNS.notes: JNS founded 2014; explicitly pro-Israel editorial perspective.

Democracy Now!.ownership: Non-profit, listener/viewer-supported. No corporate or government ownership.
Democracy Now!.notes: Founded 1996 by Amy Goodman; independent progressive daily news program.

Politico Europe.ownership: Owned by Axel Springer SE (German media conglomerate, majority-owned by KKR since 2019).
Politico Europe.notes: Brussels-based EU-politics outlet; European edition of Politico, acquired by Axel Springer in 2021.

The Telegraph.ownership: Owned by Telegraph Media Group; control passed to RedBird Capital Partners (US investment firm) in 2025 after the RedBird IMI ownership dispute.
The Telegraph.notes: UK broadsheet, historically aligned with the Conservative Party.

Fox News.ownership: Owned by Fox Corporation, controlled by the Murdoch family trust.
Fox News.notes: Highest-rated US cable news network; opinion programming leans strongly right of its news desk.

The New York Times.ownership: Owned by The New York Times Company, publicly traded; the Ochs-Sulzberger family holds controlling Class B shares.
The New York Times.notes: Largest US newspaper by digital subscribers.

AP News.ownership: The Associated Press is a non-profit cooperative owned by its member newspapers and broadcasters.
AP News.notes: Wire service; content syndicated across thousands of outlets.

New York Post.ownership: Owned by News Corp, controlled by the Murdoch family trust.
New York Post.notes: Oldest continuously published US daily; tabloid format.

The Washington Times.ownership: Owned by Operations Holdings, affiliated with the Unification Church (founded by Sun Myung Moon).
The Washington Times.notes: Washington DC daily, conservative editorial line since its 1982 founding.

MercoPress.ownership: Independent news agency based in Montevideo, Uruguay; privately held.
MercoPress.notes: Covers South Atlantic / Mercosur region in English and Spanish.

South China Morning Post.ownership: Owned by Alibaba Group (Chinese e-commerce conglomerate) since 2016.
South China Morning Post.notes: Hong Kong English-language daily; editorial independence debated since the Alibaba acquisition — ownership is the relevant disclosure.

The Japan Times.ownership: Owned by News2u Holdings (Japanese PR/communications group) since 2017.

Nikkei Asia.ownership: Owned by Nikkei Inc., an employee-owned Japanese media company (also owner of the Financial Times).
Nikkei Asia.notes: English-language pan-Asian business and news coverage.

The Straits Times.ownership: Published by SPH Media Trust, a Singapore not-for-profit backed by government funding since the 2021 restructuring.
The Straits Times.notes: Singapore's paper of record; operates under Singapore's press regulatory regime — state-adjacent by structure.

The Times of India.ownership: Owned by Bennett, Coleman & Co. (The Times Group), controlled by the Jain family.
